key_insight: WeldMamba predicts future weld-pool geometry from past images, process settings, and electrical signals to enable lookahead segmentation for delayed closed-loop welding control.
engineering_relevance: This gives laser wire-feed welding systems a faster, physics-informed perception layer for anticipating keyhole, wire, and molten-pool states before actuator lag becomes critical.
method: Historical coaxial grayscale images are fused with welding parameters and wire-state electrical signals.
A spatiotemporal state space model performs patch-level temporal reasoning with horizon-conditioned latent prediction.
Motion-aware decoding plus SDF, temporal-consistency, and distillation losses constrain future mask geometry.
evidence_signal: On a 43-sequence laser welding dataset, the method achieved 74.63% mIoU at a 500 ms prediction horizon.
watch_item: Performance depends on aligned multimodal inputs and a specific welding setup, so robustness under different sensors, materials, or larger delay shifts still needs validation.
paper_title: Physics-Guided Spatiotemporal State Space Modeling for Lookahead Molten Pool Segmentation in Laser Wire-Feed Welding
